title: Oreocarya holoptera
taxonomy: Oreocarya holoptera is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Oreocarya, which contains about 40 species. Oreocarya holoptera is native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The specific classification of Oreocarya holoptera is still evolving as new research emerges. Further studies are needed to confirm its exact taxonomic classification within the broader plant kingdom.

intro: Oreocarya holoptera, also known as the "high-altitude Oreocarya," is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). The health benefits of Oreocarya holoptera include its potential to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine for wound healing, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of Oreocarya holoptera include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Oreocarya holoptera is commonly prepared as a tea or infused in oil to create a topical treatment for skin irritations and wounds.
